items.title: Private Credit Mood Is Stronger Than Reported, Arcmont CEO Says
items.summary: Institutional investors are showing faith in private credit even as retail money gets spooked, since default rates in portfolios remain low, according to Anthony Fobel, chief executive officer of Arcmont Asset Management.

items.title: UAE Sells Bumper Gulf Oil Volumes for Loading Over Coming Months
items.summary: Abu Dhabi National Oil Co. is selling a bumper volume of crude originating from inside the Persian Gulf to traders and refiners in Asia, in the first tenders of their kind for such grades since the Iran war began.

items.title: China’s Xi Jinping arrives in a North Korea armed with advanced warships and AI drones
items.summary:
When Chinese President Xi Jinping arrived in Pyongyang on Monday, he was entering a country undergoing notable development in both its nuclear programme and conventional combat capacity.

items.title: Trump Says Iran, Israel Looking to Do ‘Immediate Ceasefire’
items.summary: US President Donald Trump said Israel and Iran are looking to agree to an immediate ceasefire following a series of strikes, while Iran’s central military command says the military operation against Israel has ended, according to semi-official Fars news agency. Tyler Kendall reports on Bloomberg Television. (Source: Bloomberg)

Qatar Quietly Sends LNG Tanker Through Hormuz Despite Tensions
items.summary: Another liquefied natural gas shipment from Qatar transited the Strait of Hormuz over the weekend, according to ship data, even as tension in the region threatens a broader peace deal between the US and Iran.

items.title: Israel, Iran Exchange Missile Attacks
items.summary: Israel said it struck several military targets in Iran, retaliating against missile attacks by Tehran despite President Donald Trump’s call for Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu to refrain from hitting back. (Source: Bloomberg)

items.title: Kim Yo Jong Rejects Denuclearisation as North Korea Expands Nuclear Arsenal
Xi lands in North Korea with denuclearisation likely off the table
items.summary:
Chinese President Xi Jinping started his first visit to North Korea since 2019 pledging “unwavering” friendship and deeper ties with a country that boasts a growing nuclear arsenal and increasing ties with Russia.
